entries.title: Hackathon Champion
body: First place out of <mark class="hl">48 competing teams</mark>. Built a full-stack app in <mark class="hl--cyan">24 hours</mark> that [brief description of what it solved]. Judged on technical execution, UX, and real-world impact.
Read the project write-up ↗ or view the source code on GitHub ↗.

entries.title: First SaaS Product Shipped
body: Deployed the first version of [Product Name] and reached <mark class="hl">[X] paying users</mark> within the first week. Grew to <mark class="hl--green">[Y] users</mark> in 3 months with zero ad spend.
Built end-to-end solo: backend, frontend, payments via Stripe ↗, and infrastructure on AWS. Visit the product ↗

entries.title: Bachelor's Degree — Computer Science
body: Graduated with honors, GPA X.X / 4.0. Thesis: "[Your Thesis Title]" — supervised by Prof. [Name] ↗.
Relevant coursework: Algorithms, Distributed Systems, Machine Learning, Compilers. Member of the ACM Student Chapter ↗.

entries.title: AWS Solutions Architect — Associate
body: Passed with a score of <mark class="hl">[XXX] / 1000</mark>. Covers cloud architecture design, high availability, fault tolerance, and cost optimization on AWS.
Verify credential: Credly badge ↗  ·  About the cert ↗

entries.title: Software Engineer
body: Working on [brief description of team/product]. Key contributions: [what you shipped], improving <mark class="hl--cyan">system performance by [X]%</mark> and reducing latency from [A]ms to <mark class="hl">[B]ms</mark>.
Stack: [Tech 1], [Tech 2], [Tech 3]. Learn more at [company].com ↗.

entries.title: Open Source Contribution Merged
body: PR merged into [Project Name] ↗, a project with 200+ GitHub stars. The change [brief description] improved performance by ~30% in [specific scenario].
View the pull request ↗

entries.title: Software Engineering Intern
body: Summer internship on the [team name] team. Built <mark class="hl">[feature name]</mark> — shipped to production in 10 weeks, used by <mark class="hl--cyan">[X]k users</mark>.
Presented work at the company-wide intern demo day to an audience of 200+ engineers. See the project ↗

entries.title: PixelRogue — Game Jam Entry
body: Built a complete browser-based roguelike in <mark class="hl">72 hours</mark> — procedurally generated dungeons, turn-based combat, and <mark class="hl--cyan">30+ enemy types</mark>.
Ranked [X] out of [Y] submissions. Play it in your browser ↗  ·  Source ↗
